e Site Map for navigation links Communication Synthesis Infrastructure Homepage Readings Download Installation Contacts CosiNoc-1.2 The first version of COSI was 1.2. This version is restricted to the synthesis of on-chip networks. It is an easy-to

synthesis of on-chip networks. It is an easy-to-install release that implements an entire design flow, from the specification of the communication requirements of a System-on-Chip to the generation of the SystemC implementation of an optimal

tion of the SystemC implementation of an optimal Network-on-Chip. CosiNoc 1.2 (binary release for Fedora Core 6). User manual in pdf format. COSI 2.0 Library Version 2.0 of COSI has a radically different software architecture than its previous ve

erent software architecture than its previous version. This is a library that consists of: A data structure to represent labeled graph with generic labels. A set of models of network components. Input/output services to parse the problem specif

Input/output services to parse the problem specification and to generate synthesis results. A set of optimization algorithms for network problems. The library include two "plug-ins": one for the synthesis of On-Chip Communications (OCC) and on

synthesis of On-Chip Communications (OCC) and one for the synthesis of Building Automation Networks (BAN). COSI 2.0 Library (c++ source code, platform independent) COSI 2.0 Library (MS Visual C++ project by Xiaohang Wang ) User manual (generate b

project by Xiaohang Wang ) User manual (generate by doxygen). Example of main function for OCC synthesis The services provided by the COSI library can be used by a program to define synthesis flows. An example of main function for the synthesis

. An example of main function for the synthesis of OCCs can be downloaded here . Example of main function for BAN synthesis The services provided by the COSI library can be used by a program to define synthesis flows. An example of main functio
define synthesis flows. An example of main function for the synthesis of BANs can be downloaded here . © 2008 COSI Contact ©2002-2018 U.C. Regents
